LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS ATRIUM MEDICAL CORPORATION, MYCHALL MORRIS & JONATHAN LUNG'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO MARCH 13, 2014. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED.
MINI MINUTES FOR FEB-26-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-13-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING ON DEFENDANTS ATRIUM MEDICAL CORPORATION, MYCHALL MORRIS AND JONATHAN LUNG'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - SUSTAINED. (CCP SEC. 430.10(E).) AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ("FAC") DOES NOT PLEAD FACTS ESTABLISHING CAUSATION. AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE FAC DOES NOT PLEAD FACTS ESTABLISHING THAT DEFENDANTS ARE PHYSICIANS OR SURGEONS, AND THEREFORE DOES NOT STATE A CLAIM FOR MEDICAL BATTERY. (BAJI 6.10.5.) THE FAC DOES NOT PLEAD FACTS ESTABLISHING THAT DEFENDANTS INTENTIONALLY DID AN ACT THAT RESULTED IN A HARMFUL OR OFFENSIVE CONTACT WITH PATRICIA JAMYE'S PERSON. (BAJI 7.50.) AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE FAC ALLEGES THAT DEFENDANTS ARE MEDICAL DEVICE REPRESENTATIVES, NOT PROFESSIONAL HEALTHCARE PROVIDERS. (PREVAILING PARTY SUBMITTED A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH MODIFICATION: DEFENDANTS ATRIUM MEDICAL CORPORATION, MYCHALL MORRIS AND JONATHAN LUNG'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR STRICT LIABILITY, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT ("SAC") DOES NOT PLEAD THAT THE MESH PRODUCT WAS INHERENTLY DANGEROUS WITHOUT INSTRUCTIONS. (DELEON V. COMMERCIAL MANUFACTURING SUPPLY (1983) 148 CAL.APP.3D 336, 343.) FURTHER, PLAINTIFFS CITE NO AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT A MEDICAL DEVICE MANUFACTURER CAN BE HELD STRICTLY LIABLE FOR NOT TRAINING A PHYSICIAN ON HOW TO USE A DEVICE. AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. THE SAC PLEADS THAT THE DEFENDANT MEDICAL DEVICE REPRESENTATIVES "PARTICIPATED" IN THE "SURGICAL PROCEDURE." (SAC, PAR. 11.) BECAUSE DEFENDANTS PARTICIPATED IN THE SURGERY, DEFENDANTS OWED A DUTY TO THE DECEDENT. THE SAC FURTHER PLEADS THAT DEFENDANTS' PARTICIPATION IN THE SURGERY "CAUSED DECEDENT'S DEATH." (SAC, PAR. 11.) THIS SUFFICIENTLY PLEADS CAUSATION. AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR RIGHT TO PRIVACY, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. THE SAC ADEQUATELY PLEADS THAT DEFENDANTS VIOLATED THE DECEDENT'S RIGHT TO PRIVACY BY BEING PRESENT DURING THE SURGERY WITHOUT HER CONSENT. (SAC, PARS. 11, 27.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.
ORDER RE: DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS ATRIUM MEDICAL CORPORATION, MYCHALL MORRIS, AND JONATHAN LUNG'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFFS STILL DO NOT CITE ANY AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT A MEDICAL DEVICE MANUFACTURER CAN BE HELD STRICTLY LIABLE TO A PATIENT FOR NOT TRAINING THE PHYSICIAN ON HOW TO USE A DEVICE. ADDITIONALLY, PLAINTIFFS' OPPOSITION DOES NOT ADDRESS OR DISTINGUISH THE HOLDING IN CARLIN V. SUPERIOR COURT (1996) 13 CAL.4TH 1104, 1116, WHICH FOUND THAT THE DUTY OF A MEDICAL MANUFACTURER TO WARN RUNS TO THE DOCTOR, NOT TO THE PATIENT. FURTHER, IN PLENGER V. ALZA CORP. (1992) 11 CAL.APP.4TH 349, 362, THE COURT STATES THAT "[W]E ARE AWARE OF NO AUTHORITY WHICH REQUIRES A MANUFACTURER TO WARN OF A RISK WHICH IS READILY KNOWN AND APPARENT TO THE CONSUMER, IN THIS CASE THE PHYSICIAN." WHETHER TRAINING IS REQUIRED BY A PARTICULAR SURGEON PRIOR TO USING A NEW PRODUCT DURING SURGERY SHOULD BE APPARENT TO THAT SURGEON AND DOES NOT REQUIRE THE MANUFACTURER OR ITS SALES REPRESENTATIVE TO PROVIDE A SPECIFIC WARNING ABOUT TRAINING. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND IN LIGHT OF THE FACT PLAINTIFFS HAVE HAD MULTIPLE OPPORTUNITIES TO AMEND AND PLAINTIFFS ASSERT NO FACTS IN THEIR OPPOSITION ABOUT HOW THEY COULD AMEND TO STATE A VIABLE CAUSE OF ACTION. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/EHG)
DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION OF ALL PARTIES AND ALL CAUSES OF ACTION WITH PREJUDICE (TRANSACTION ID # 56472724)

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-15-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: Atty. KAREN P. CICCONE of Akerman, LLP, Tel No. (213) 688-9500 appearing for Defendants Atrium Medical Corporation, Mychall Morris, and Jonathan Lung;
No other appearances made on this matter.

RULING - The matter was not argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ATRIUM MEDICAL CORPORATION, MYCHALL MORRIS, AND JONATHAN LUNG'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiffs still do not cite any authority for the proposition that a medical device manufacturer can be held strictly liable to a patient for not training the physician on how to use a device. Additionally, Plaintiffs' opposition does not address or distinguish the holding in Carlin v. Superior Court (1996) 13 Cal.4th 1104, 1116, which found that the duty of a medical manufacturer to warn runs to the doctor, not to the patient. Further, in Plenger v. Alza Corp. (1992) 11 Cal.App.4th 349, 362, the court states that "[w]e are aware of no authority which requires a manufacturer to warn of a risk which is readily known and apparent to the consumer, in this case the physician." Whether training is required by a particular surgeon prior to using a new product during surgery should be apparent to that surgeon and does not require the manufacturer or its sales representative to provide a specific warning about training. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend in light of the fact Plaintiffs have had multiple opportunities to amend and Plaintiffs assert no facts in their opposition about how they could amend to state a viable cause of action. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-12-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Burton, Esq., 415.441.5544, for plaintiffs Samuel Jayme, Joseph Jayme and Ramon Jayme, Sr.
Karen Ciccone, Esq., 213.688.9500, for defendants Atrium Medical Corporation, Mychall Morris and Jonathan Lung.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling with modification: DEFENDANTS ATRIUM MEDICAL CORPORATION, MYCHALL MORRIS AND JONATHAN LUNG'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. As to the first cause of action for Strict Liability, the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. The Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") does not plead that the mesh product was inherently dangerous without instructions. (DeLeon v. Commercial Manufacturing Supply (1983) 148 Cal.App.3d 336, 343.) Further, Plaintiffs cite no authority for the proposition that a medical device manufacturer can be held strictly liable for not training a physician on how to use a device. As to the second cause of action for Negligence, the demurrer is overruled. The SAC pleads that the Defendant medical device representatives "participated" in the "surgical procedure." (SAC, par. 11.) Because Defendants participated in the surgery, Defendants owed a duty to the decedent. The SAC further pleads that Defendants' participation in the surgery "caused decedent's death." (SAC, par. 11.) This sufficiently pleads causation. As to the third cause of action for Right to Privacy, the demurrer is overruled. The SAC adequately pleads that Defendants violated the decedent's right to privacy by being present during the surgery without her consent. (SAC, pars. 11, 27.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-02-2014 Continued To Jun-18-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-13-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Karen P. Ciccone, Esq. of Akerman LLP, Tel. 213.688.9500, for defendants and moving party via Court Call;
Mark Burton, Jr., Esq., of Hersh and Hersh, Tel. 415.441.5544, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANTs ATRIUM MEDICAL CORPORATION, MYCHALL MORRIS and JONATHAN LUNG's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - Sustained. (CCP sec. 430.10(e).) As to the first cause of action, the demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. The First Amended Complaint ("FAC") does not plead facts establishing causation. As to the second cause of action, the demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. The FAC does not plead facts establishing that Defendants are physicians or surgeons, and therefore does not state a claim for medical battery. (BAJI 6.10.5.) The FAC does not plead facts establishing that Defendants intentionally did an act that resulted in a harmful or offensive contact with Patricia Jamye's person. (BAJI 7.50.) As to the third cause of action, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The FAC alleges that Defendants are medical device representatives, not professional healthcare providers. (Prevailing party submitted a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.
